item.title: Comic Dump of my Dog- he needs help
item.description:
Atreyu has been in a lot of my comics. He’s my best friend and is such a joy in my life
He goes with us on all our adventures, never missing a moment to live his best life and have fun.
He was a shelter rescue that was abused and starved by his previous owners before we adopted and rehabilitated him
He is my EVERYTHING. And he almost died last night. If my comics have ever made you laugh, smile, anything, I pray you will help me get exposure on this post. We are desperate. Thank you
We need help. On May 11th, at 11:30 pm, our beloved German Shepherd, Atreyu, had his stomach flip; Gastric Dilatation Volvulus (GDV). This is an emergency and life-threatening situation in which a dog with this condition has on average two hours to live. Last night, while feeding my foster kitten, I checked on Atreyu, who had seemed slightly off the hour before. His abdomen had swelled up to three or four times the size it should be. I knew instantly his stomach had flipped and he was nearing death. Freddie was able to speed to the Veterinary ER Hospital and get there in just enough time for them to perform the life-saving surgery. Atreyu isn’t out of the woods yet, he will be hospitalized for a few more days. He might still pass away but we are hoping he survives. The vets are optimistic. The full vet bill is around 4,500 right now, they warned us it will probably go up each day he’s there. They estimated it could range towards 6,000 but they have no way of knowing. We have a small amount in our savings but we are not able to afford the full amount. Any help from our friends and family or even fans of the comics, to help pay towards his emergency surgery would be desperately and greatly appreciated. I know we are not the only ones tight on money in this economy so please know, no matter what amount you can pay/or upvote this post, if you are able to help us we are forever thankful for anything you can do. http://spot.fund/vnr8hsc
item.dc:creator: ristay

exactly what I pictured
hedgie
dang

us guys see this and think "hell yeah"
as is tradition

um, yeah
i got nothin'
that's my set.. have a great night gang!! happy mothers day to the moms of imgur!

item.title: Welp, guess I'm committed now
item.description:
Woke up this morning to find my post has exploded! Did not see that coming...
Figure I'll share my second project I've made!
It's a....ghost?
A blob? That wants my soul?
Oh fuck it can fly!!!
It's a mothman!
I'm so happy with how the wings came out! There was much cursing as I was trying to figure it out.
L̴̵̨̺̣͕̬͍̺̣̬̥͈̂͗̀ͤ͂͂ͯ̆͗̆͘͡_̀ A͖̞̙̬͈̖͑̏ͯ͛̊̉͒ͧ̒̕̚ Ḿ̴̴̴̴̡̪͚̩̫̖̣̦̝͐̓̓̆̀̈́͊̌ͯ͠ P̵͔͉̥̩͉̬̞̗̈́ͣ̏̀̄̍̽̏̆ͣ̾͟͞͝
I was crying as I read all your amazing words! I can't wait to post more for you all to see!
item.dc:creator: hookedonyarnics

item.title: I got an apartment! Living on my own terms for the first time in 7 years!
item.description:
It's not much, but it's mine! I can wake up when I want, I can go to bed when I want. I can eat whatever I want whenever I want. I can say what I want; I can sing what I want.
Biggest downside is this tight tiny kitchen. I LOVE to cook. But now that I'm living alone I'll take a bit of a break from that to explore other creative outlets. Cooking for 1 (who am I kidding? I am 2) is not easy and not usually worth the work/clean up
OMG a bedroom all of my own??? I can fit a big ole comfy bed in here!
bathroom's nothing fancy, but it serves its purpose and is not horrifying (horrifying bathrooms were a common thread in my apartment search....don't know why more landlords don't realize it's an immediate "no thanks"). will definitely be upgrading the shower head asap.
literally everything I owned on my move in date fits in this closet. and the only thing I've bought since then is food and a queen bed.
other side of the closet; love the shelves for a makeshift linen closet
this is what sold me on the property... < 20 min walk to the beach. I went today for the first time; it took me about 25 mins, but I was taking it super easy (recuperating from surgery) and in flip flops.
looking forward to many morning walks on the beach
something's always got to go wrong....the kitchen sink doesn't drain worth a damn. I'll be submitting a maintenance request first thing tomorrow. (it's not ruining my day, and I don't want to ruin anyone elses's Sunday)
Kitchen set up so far. It hasn't been as difficult to work with as I expected.
here is my faaaaannnnncccccy bed (by fancy I mean "not a twin bed", can you even imagine??!!!). I saved a LOT by purchasing the floor model, also. And their credit check must be weak af because they gave me 0% financing for a year. I'm no longer sleeping in a child's bed; I've slept in worse; beggars can't be choosers. p.s. - stay tuned for the story of Fluffy (the yellow *stuffed animal* in this pic). I have been meaning to make a post about Fluffy for quite some time. She's my ride or die for like 38 fucking years (that cannot possibly be right....FUCK ME THAT'S RIGHT)
Don't judge me - I moved in a rush and am also very lazy and procrastinate a lot. Just illustrating further that almost everything I own fits in this closet. It's insane.
the bare necessities (sing it!). crosswords help me poop
this is my current wfh set up (which is at least as good, if not better, than it has been for the last 4 years). hopefully improving in the next 6 months. Edit: You guys are AMAZING!! Thank you so much for all the love, support, and tips! I am so excited, and it makes me so happy that you all are so happy for me! Love this community so much! I was originally going to post a whole long story about the last few years of my life, but I feel like it doesn't even matter now. Looking ahead with positivity!
item.dc:creator: butnoelephants

She loves goats
We had an in-depth conversation about this book when she recently reread it and was questioning her school

item.title: I thought I was good.
item.description: Went feral after getting hammered, called a bunch of people, showed up a couple places. Finally got some coke and literally blew through 40 bucks in 2 lines. I did the right thing and texted my people after I woke up and realized what happened. So much support. And I think there won't be a next time allowed. They care too much.
item.dc:creator: kitenkatt

item.title: So this is love
item.description: My best friend. I don't know what I would do without him. Especially on days like today when it's Mother's Day and I'm sad and missing my mom so much. I hope this doesn't break the no selfies except on Christmas rule. Edit: Didn't expect to make the front page. Donate to an animal rescue if you can and want to. Chase is a rescue that was previously found as a stray.
item.dc:creator: MonstreBelle

item.title: So much for relaxing, I have a special project! A 1911 Series 400 National Cash Register! Wooo!
item.description:
The holy grail! Well for me anyway! Early this week I lucked out and caught this monster right as it was listed for sale. Normally I don't bite on these because they're too expensive. They're big, iconic, and collectible. Normally they sit around $800 in the condition you're seeing up there, and that's assuming they're intact and haven't been used for parts. I lucked out big time and got this big girl for a -song- at $250!
My first question for the seller was, "What's wrong with it?" There had to be a reason they were lowballing their own asking price that much. He said it's broken, but doesn't quite know where. Just that it doesn't work. I thought about it for a few... milliseconds. >_> "Broken" can mean a lot of things from gummed up to missing critical, irreplaceable parts internally. I decided to to take a gamble and drove 2 hours just outside Wisconsin Dells to pick it up from the barbershop it had been in use in since the 1960s. The shop's owner, a ninety year old guy, and his son who was doing the selling both met me there and we made small talk until the older gentleman promptly started pushing buttons to show me how it worked. Two cranks on the handle and everything worked fine as far as I could tell, and the son looked like he was going to pop. Apparently he never looked into what "broken" meant. Broken, in this case, means the drawer doesn't stay shut. I poker faced as hard as I could poker, because I had no business buying this machine for what they were asking. :3
The main reason I want one of these - and you best believe I'm keeping this one - is because the hand cranked models are just *cool* in my book. There's no main spring to drive the interior, no complicated electrics, just hand power and precision gearing.
The 400 series, National Cash Register's line of newly designed registers with a "4XX" model number, was their first departure from lever actuated machines with pop-up price flags. Inside the case are numbered wheels (think The Price is Right's big wheel) that line up through a small window. To help customers locate their total with the unfamiliar layout, they added the iconic pointing finger wearing a suit cuff. I don't know for certain if it originated with NCR or if they borrowed it from common signage, but it's a neat little addition that helped makes this model stand out.
Against all odds, the original marble coin tap is intact. I'm used to seeing these split in two! Also, you can see how the drawer sits askew. It's partly due to it not closing properly, and the track it follows having broken away from the frame. Those are repairs I'll have to tackle first, but which I feel confident will be straightforward.
You may have also noticed that the front faceplate was unscrewed. I was worried when I noticed it at the barbershop, but the owner kept the screws safe after removing the plate to try brushing hair clippings out from behind the buttons. @.@ I had to blow out the whole thing in my driveway, it had so much hair on it.
Here's a better shot of some of the damage I'll be fixing. The base split along a joint that I'll be cleaning and gluing back together. The metal biscuit is still in there, so I'll have to clamp the whole thing when I start on it. On the top of the receipt printer cage, the top curve is a bit dented in as well. It should line up with the body of the register. A bit of rubber malleting on some padding should reshape it, hopefully.
Quick back shot. This side is what customers would see, though in the barbershop it faced a wall.
I'm most excited by the condition of the brass. It's dirty and tarnished, but otherwise protected. I haven't found any green death where it got eaten away, so if there is any it's sparse!
Just a little dent.
And a little split.
And a bit of former tinkering.
But hey, I got the screws!
The back of the cash drawer pretty quickly tells me why it won't shut. Ignoring that busted spring I need to replace, there's a whole piece of hardware missing from the center! I wonder where it went...
There it is! Looks like it got ripped clear off the drawer. That's going to be tricky, because if it stripped out the screws then I need to talk to a woodworker on how I can reattach it through the old holes. I'm guessing a larger diameter screw, but first I'll talk to some pros.
Fun shot of the drawer front. I love how they stylized their logo in the early years. ^^
Inside the cage is the receipt printer! The owner said he used to have paper for it, so I have high hopes that it'll still print. Just need to find the dimensions for the paper, the spool diameter, and some non-glossy paper...
And here's how this "broken" register works! The cashier had to select their assigned letter (I randomly chose "D"), then enter the dollar, ten cent, and single cent value of what was being bought. The register defaults to Cash totals unless otherwise specified. I chose "Return on Account" for kicks. After that, two full cranks on the handle and you're done!
For the extra curious, here's the receipt printer operating during the crank cycle... minus paper. :)
I don't care what they say, I'm going to break this register down and build it back up in better condition than it's been in for decades! This restoration is going to take more time than usual, I wager, because I'll be learning the disassembly steps for the first time all over again! ^_^ Wish me luck! I'll post updates as progress is made! (If you want to help offset some of the cost of these restorations, I have a ko-fi! Ko-fi.com/elbowdeepinahorse)
item.dc:creator: ElbowDeepInAHorse

item.title: Monday Music Meme Dump
item.description:
This is just a sample
Some music memes for Monday
I can hear this in my head
What even are words?
Live your best life
Beats on in the aftelife
Vibe check
Stairway
What?
Oh yeah
Right back to it
We got the blues up in here
WXPN on in the background
Who knew?
What are you listening to?
Cash wants to know. He’s serious, don’t let him down. #dogtax

item.title: Havasupai Falls, Arizona. First time backpacking. Epic, bucket list shit
item.description:
Mooney Falls
Yay! We made it
On the way down to Mooney Falls
Between Mooney Falls and Beaver Falls
Beautiful ivy
Ladder
NOT 5 minutes away...
Climbing down the ladder at Mooney Falls was just the beginning hahaha
More ladders. Doesn't look the least bit sketchy although it was secure
Feel the burn
Beaver Falls. Absolutely beautiful
FINALLY! Next to Beaver falls.
Beaver falls
Still beaver falls
Going back the way we came
So beautiful
Heading back up at Mooney Falls to get back to the campground. Sisnt get any pictures going down
Havasu Falls
Warning labels lol
Little tidbit of information
50 foot falls.
item.dc:creator: jackieblahblahblah

item.title: Here's some bottom shelf memes, Mom.
item.description:
OddOnesOut because some fuck cropped out his signature
@HileyNoteson Yoooooooooooooooooooooo
Still unsure about joining the Bottom Shelf Community server? Check out our museum of stupidity. https://imgur.com/gallery/FYpqqAf Join for the games! Shit, man. We play everything...if there's a specific game you like, I guarantee you'll find a shitposting meme loving Imgurian there to play it with you. Join for the memes! Imgurians share memes in the meme dumpster 24/7 Join for the Awe! Pay your pet taxes in the pet tax channel Join for the NSFW memes! Join to chat with hilarious people! Join to lurk in the corners! Join for the shockingly magnificent balance of wholesomeness and chaos. discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka
Join for the meme challenges!
twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka Saturdays & Sundays 9:30 - 11:30 p.m. CST Tuesdays - Thursdays 9:30 - 11:30 p.m. CST Follow our channel to be notified next time me, Frothy, and our favorite Imgurians from the Bottom Shelf Community go live!
Taxi Bear and Mr. Nutterbutt tax
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka

item.title: A Free Meal Day. 296 Days of free food since 2018.
item.description: Thank you @altersepp for sponsoring a meal day. Basically, you sponsored a meal for 3 zones. This is the first one. The rest will be uploaded shortly. Its is almost May and the end of the school year here in the Philippines. If you all remember, I have been handing out school supplies every class reopening. For your reference, https://imgur.com/gallery/v5fcS25 sadly this post didnt reach the FP My plan is to start a campaign for the school supplies drive to happen so I want to start as early as possible. For the school supplies, the goal is to be able to raise at least 1000$. If this goal is reached within May, Its important to buy the supplies immediately as the prices go up by June when the class is almost open. For School Supplies and Meal sponsorship, please refer to paypal ojcallope17@gmail.com. Please include which category you want me to use your money. If you prefer Wire transfer or Crypto, you may. Just let me know. You can also be patron at patreon.com/TheFreeMeaLadyImgurian to support us long term. Salamat po.
item.dc:creator: TheFreeMeaLLady

item.dc:creator: jhill

item.title: Happy Mother's Day
item.description: This pretty lady is my mom and I couldn't have asked for a better friend. Forty-five years of ups and downs and she still thinks I am her baby. She has done it all for me and I am forever grateful for her love. I wasn't an easy kid. I was sickly as a child, lots of ear infections, ear surgeries and issues with my legs that left me learning to walk in braces. She never flinched. She loved me through it and often she did it alone. As a teen, I was a punk. I fell in with a rough crowd and it was a true mess. I made so many mistakes and missteps. I too easily broke her heart on many occasions. My biggest regret is making her cry. My mom is still a pillar of strength for me. Everything I am today is what she built. My love for reading, old movies and sweet tea, all her. So, Happy Mother's Day to all the moms out there, but especially to mine.
item.dc:creator: DyceCA
